name: APC Back-UPS Pro 1500VA / 900W (BX1500M)
shortDescription: The APC BX1500M is a 1500VA/900W UPS with Automatic Voltage Regulation and 10 outlets. For AI workstation builders, it is the only way to protect hours of model fine-tuning or dataset processing from a power outage — providing enough battery runtime to save work and shut down gracefully.
useCases: Protecting fine-tuning runs and training jobs from power loss
Safe graceful shutdown during outages for always-on AI servers
Voltage regulation for sensitive GPU and Mini PC hardware
Any AI workstation build — GPU rigs, Mac Minis, Mini PCs
Home lab setups with 24/7 uptime requirements
pros: 900W capacity — handles RTX 5070 system under full AI inference load
Automatic Voltage Regulation (AVR) — protects hardware from voltage sags and surges
10 outlets (5 battery + 5 surge) — powers full workstation setup
LCD display — shows load percentage, estimated runtime, battery health
USB connectivity for automatic graceful OS shutdown on power loss
cons: 900W max limits simultaneous GPU + monitor + NAS draw at full load
Bulky — needs floor or shelf space near the workstation
verdict: The APC BX1500M is the most important accessory in this entire catalog — and the most overlooked. Every minute of a fine-tuning run or dataset processing job is compute time that cannot be recovered if the power flickers. The BX1500M provides 5–15 minutes of battery runtime at typical AI workstation loads, enough for any OS to detect the power loss, save state, and shut down cleanly via the USB connection. Buy this before you start your first serious training job.

faq.question: Will the APC BX1500M handle an RTX 5070 system under load?
faq.answer: Yes. The RTX 5070 draws ~150W under full AI inference load. A full system (GPU + CPU + memory + drives + monitor) typically draws 300–500W total. The BX1500M's 900W capacity provides comfortable headroom. At 400W draw, it provides approximately 8–12 minutes of battery runtime — enough to save progress and shut down gracefully.

faq.question: How does the UPS protect AI training runs?
faq.answer: The BX1500M connects to your PC via USB and works with APC PowerChute or native OS UPS support (Windows and macOS both detect it). When power fails, the OS receives a signal and can trigger an automatic graceful shutdown script — saving model checkpoints, pausing training, and writing logs before the battery runs out. Without this, a power cut means corrupted weights and lost hours of work.

faq.question: Does the APC BX1500M work with a Mac Mini?
faq.answer: Yes. macOS natively supports UPS devices connected via USB. Go to System Settings → Battery → UPS and configure shutdown behavior. When power fails, macOS will automatically save state and shut down the Mac Mini before the battery depletes. The BX1500M's 900W capacity is more than enough for any Mac Mini setup — the M4 Pro draws under 40W at peak.
